title: Throne-chariot vision
summary_text: Ezekiel sees a storm from the north, living creatures, wheels within wheels, and the likeness of the glory of the LORD. He falls on his face as the Spirit lifts him and sends him to rebellious Israel. The vision shows that God is present in exile and sovereign over every throne.
key_points: Ezekiel sees a storm from the north, living creatures, wheels within wheels, and the likeness of the glory of the LORD.
He falls on his face as the Spirit lifts him and sends him to rebellious Israel.
The vision shows that God is present in exile and sovereign over every throne.

title: Commission to rebellious Israel
summary_text: God sends Ezekiel to a stubborn and hard-hearted house and gives him a scroll to eat. Whether they listen or refuse, he must speak the LORD's words without fear. The sweet scroll shows that prophecy is both burden and gift.

title: Vision of the future temple
summary_text: Ezekiel is taken to a high mountain and shown a measuring man and the outer courts of a future temple. The careful details signal order, holiness, and divine attention to worship. The final vision begins with measured hope.

title: The inner temple measured
summary_text: The sanctuary and surrounding chambers are measured with care. The intricate layout emphasizes separation, beauty, and sacred order. God's presence is not casual, so the house is built with precision.

title: Chambers for the priests
summary_text: The outer buildings and priestly chambers are arranged for holy use. The design keeps the sacred space distinct from common life. The chapter reinforces the seriousness of holiness near God's house.

title: Glory returns to the temple
summary_text: The glory of the LORD returns from the east and fills the house as Ezekiel falls before it. God declares that this is the place of his throne and gives instructions for the altar. Restoration reaches its climax in renewed divine presence.

title: Priests, prince, and holy gates
summary_text: The eastern gate remains shut because the LORD entered by it, and only the prince may sit in the outer court. Foreigners are restricted from sanctuary service, and the priests who went astray are corrected. Worship is guarded from old compromises.
key_points: The eastern gate remains shut because the LORD entered by it, and only the prince may sit in the outer court.

title: Holy portions and just measures
summary_text: The land is apportioned for the sanctuary, priests, Levites, the city, and the prince. Offerings are regulated, and dishonest scales are rejected. Civic justice and temple holiness are bound together.

title: Worship schedules and offerings
summary_text: The prince enters by appointed gates on sabbaths and new moons, and offerings are supplied in a regular pattern. The priests prepare the sacrifices, and property rules prevent exploitation. Ordered worship replaces the corruption of the past.

title: River from the temple
summary_text: Water flows from beneath the temple threshold and becomes a river that heals the Dead Sea. Fish multiply, trees bear fruit, and the land on both sides is restored. Life spreads outward from God's presence.

title: The tribal allotments and the city's name
summary_text: The tribes receive their portions in orderly bands from north to south, with a central holy district for the LORD. The city is measured and named for God's presence. The book closes by saying that the LORD is there.
